config_changes: none — no .cargo/config.toml, Cargo.toml, feature-attr, or target-spec change. The `cargo kbuild` alias already builds `-Zbuild-std=core,compiler_builtins,alloc` (so `alloc` is present), and M7 reuses the existing M6 `frame_alloc`/`frame_free` facade, the M3 typed `mmu` layer (`PageTable512`/`level_index`/`make_entry`/`entry_addr`/`entry_is_valid`/`ENTRIES`/`PAGE_SIZE`/`SHIFT_*`), and the M5 `heap` module. No new dependency or build flag is needed.
design_choice: GROWABLE-ON-DEMAND (the preferred design; `HeapState::grow` is invoked from `HeapState::alloc` on a first-fit miss, calling the per-arch tb-hal primitive `crate::arch::map_heap_frames`). `alloc()` loops one verbatim-M5 first-fit pass (`try_fit`) → on miss `grow(min_bytes)` → retry; only when growth makes no progress (window disabled / window VA exhausted / true physical-frame OOM) does `alloc` return null. Window VA per arch: x86_64 = 0x0000_0100_0000_0000 (PML4[2] — boot uses PML4[0], M4 `user` PML4[1]; PML4[2] is provably vacant); aarch64 = 0x1_0000_0000 (L1[4] — identity L1[0..1], M3 self-test L1[2], M4 `user` L1[3]; L1[4] is provably vacant, and 4 GiB < 2^39 so it is inside the T0SZ=25 VA space). Both windows are kernel-only (no U/S on x86; AP=EL1-RW, no EL0 on aarch64), RW + NX (x86 PTE bit63 with EFER.NXE already set by M3; aarch64 PXN|UXN), OUTSIDE the identity map, and capped at 1 GiB of VA (one PDPT[0]→PD subtree on x86 / one L2 subtree on aarch64), while the walk stays fully general so it builds/reuses intermediate tables on demand. FRAME GIVE-BACK / criterion (c): the heap NEVER shrinks or unmaps — the hard rule "the M5 allocator ALGEBRA stays exactly as M5 wrote it; ONLY the backing store + a grow hook change" forbids adding an unmap/free path to `dealloc`, so the window legitimately RETAINS its mapped frames for reuse. This is the spec-sanctioned "a window legitimately keeps its frames; say so" case: no-leak is proven at the ALLOCATOR level (heap used-bytes returns to baseline after every drop) and the retained frames are proven REUSABLE — the self-test serves a SECOND 4 MiB allocation with `pmm_free_frames()` unchanged, i.e. retention is reuse, not loss. M10 implication: this is exactly the higher-half-style kernel-only window M10's per-agent address spaces need — M10's `AddressSpace` = fresh top-level table (M6 frames, M3 typed layer) that keeps THIS kernel window mapped across every CR3/TTBR0 switch; the per-arch `heap_window()` constant and the on-demand intermediate-table builder (`ensure_table`) are the directly-reusable pieces.
rationale: CONTIGUOUS-VA GROWTH: `HeapState` keeps one monotonic `window_next` cursor; each `grow` maps a slice that BEGINS exactly at the previous slice's end, so the donated slices are VA-adjacent and `insert_free_region` coalesces them into one large CONTIGUOUS free region — which is what lets a single 4 MiB Vec (impossible in the 2 MiB .bss arena) be served over scattered physical RAM. `grow` maps `max(ceil(min_bytes/PAGE), 256)` pages (clamped to remaining window VA), so a 4 MiB request maps its whole 1024-page contiguous region in ONE `map_heap_frames` call to 1024 distinct scattered frames, donated as a single 4 MiB FreeBlock; small requests amortise over a 1 MiB chunk. INTERMEDIATE TABLES + SPLICE (per arch): `map_heap_frames` reads the live root ONCE (CR3 on x86 / TTBR0_EL1 on aarch64 — hoisted out of the per-page loop this finalization, since the root never changes mid-grow) and threads it into each per-page walk; the walk uses the M3 typed helpers (`level_index`/`make_entry`/`entry_addr`/`entry_is_valid`); `ensure_table` sources a missing interior table from M6, `zero_table`s it (M6 only left a stale free-stack link word), and splices it (P|RW on x86 / 0b11 TABLE descriptor on aarch64), REUSING already-present interior tables on later grows via the `entry_is_valid` fast path. Leaves are written kernel-only RW+NX (x86 P|RW|NX; aarch64 PAGE_KERNEL_RW_NX = page|AF|inner-SH|Normal-WB|PXN|UXN, AP=EL1-RW). Every M6 frame and the live root tables sit in the identity-mapped low RAM (x86 [0,1GiB); aarch64 the [0x4000_0000,0x8000_0000) RAM gigabyte under -m 128M/256M, all inside the M3 identity windows), so table-zeroing and leaf writes via raw identity pointers are mapped and reachable. x86 needs no `invlpg` (0→1 Present, SDM 4.10.4.3); aarch64 publishes the whole batch with one `dsb ishst; isb` (first map INVALID→valid needs no TLBI, Arm ARM D8.14). On physical-frame OOM mid-walk a just-spliced interior table is RETAINED (reused by a later grow, not leaked) and the data frame is never pulled, so a page is either fully mapped or skipped — never half-mapped; the doc comments now say exactly this. M5 ALGEBRA UNCHANGED: `try_fit` is the verbatim M5 first-fit/unlink/front+back-split/used+high_water accounting, only lifted to return `Option`; `dealloc`/`insert_free_region`/`fit`/`required` are byte-identical; `grow` is the lone new hook and is INERT until `heap_window_init` sets `window_enabled`, so M0–M5 are byte-identical (the M5 over-arena→null self-test runs before the window is enabled: `try_fit` misses → `grow` returns false because the window is disabled → `alloc` returns null, exactly as M5). DEADLOCK-FREE: `grow` runs under GLOBAL_HEAP but `map_heap_frames` touches only GLOBAL_PMM (a separate lock; `frame_alloc` is a pure free-stack pop, verified to do ZERO heap allocation) and never re-enters the heap. SELF-TEST (kernel, NO unsafe — only the safe facade + `alloc` types + number compares): M6 step (5) now RECLAIMS its exhaustion-drain pool (collected into a `with_capacity` Vec so the drain never reallocs, then freed back; asserted to return to `total`) so M7 has frames — at the script-pinned RAM (256M x86 ≈ 63k frames ≈ 0.5 MiB Vec; 128M aarch64 ≈ 32k frames ≈ 0.25 MiB Vec) this drain Vec sits well inside the 2 MiB arena; the identity clamp keeps usable RAM ≤ ~1 GiB so this stays bounded, and the comment documents the assumption. The M7 block captures the pmm baseline, `heap_window_init()`, then does a single CONTIGUOUS 4 MiB `Vec<u64>` (524288 elems, > the old 2 MiB arena) with a bijective written+read-back pattern (any mis-mapped page #PFs/data-aborts into the trap hook, which halts, so the marker never prints — a clean readback PROVES the mapping), asserts `heap_high_water > 2 MiB` + `heap_window_mapped_bytes > 0` + `pmm_free_frames` DROPPED (real frames consumed), drops it and asserts `heap_used_bytes == baseline` (no HEAP leak), then proves a SECOND 4 MiB Vec reuses the window with `pmm_free_frames` UNCHANGED (retention == reuse, the strict no-frame-leak evidence), runs a realloc-growth Vec through the unchanged first-fit/coalesce churn, and documents that `pmm_free_frames` stays below the M6 baseline because the window retains its frames (each a live PT frame or heap-owned data frame — not a leak). M0–M6 regression is proven by the same cumulative boot still printing tb-boot/M1..M6 ahead of M7. FIXES APPLIED THIS FINALIZATION: (1) the one real MAJOR — `scripts/bench-boot.sh` FINAL_MARKER advanced M6→"M7: heap OK" (+ its t_full header comment), so the vmm-boot.yml KVM boot-time read-loop times the WHOLE M7 self-test instead of stopping at M6; (2) the per-page CR3/TTBR0_EL1 re-read hoisted to one read per grow on both arches (all three reviewers' perf minor; correctness-preserving, walk logic unchanged); (3) doc accuracy on interior-table retention/no-half-mapped-page and on `heap_window_mapped_bytes` counting DATA pages only (not the intermediate page-table frames also pulled); (4) refreshed the three run-script header comments that still quoted the old marker. The write-only `window_base` field is RETAINED (it mirrors the already-shipped write-only `arena_start`, raises no `dead_code` warning, and M10 needs the window base) — verified non-issue, not churned. tb-hal builds warning-free under `#![deny(missing_docs)]` (every new pub item documented; all imported M3 helpers are used on both arches: x86 uses SHIFT_512G for the PML4 level, aarch64's 3-level walk omits it). Verified on both first-class arches (x86_64 PVH/microvm + tb-vmm/tb-boot; aarch64 virt). All edit `old_string` anchors match the PRISTINE pre-M7 (M5+M6) bytes so the parent re-applies them to a clean tree.

edits.old_string:
//! This allocator algebra is REUSED UNCHANGED by M7; only the backing store
//! ([`ARENA`] + the [`init`] call) changes there. Keep it store-agnostic.
edits.note: heap.rs 1/6: module-doc — M7 adds a frame-backed window as a second store, algebra unchanged. PRISTINE anchor.

edits.old_string:
const ARENA_BYTES: usize = 2 * 1024 * 1024;

/// In-place header at the start of every FREE region.
edits.note: heap.rs 2/6: PAGE + GROW_CHUNK_PAGES constants.
heap.rs 3/6: HeapState window fields. window_base doc now states it is retained on purpose (mirrors write-only arena_start; no dead_code warning; M10 uses it).

edits.new_string:
initialized: false,
            used: 0,
            high_water: 0,
            window_enabled: false,
            window_base: 0,
            window_next: 0,
            window_end: 0,
            window_bytes: 0,
        }
    }

    /// Lay the initial single free block over `[start, start + size)`. Idempotent
edits.note: heap.rs 4/6: const new() initialises the window fields.
heap.rs 5/6: split alloc into alloc(loop)+try_fit (verbatim M5 first-fit) and add window_init + grow. try_fit is byte-identical to M5; grow is the only new hook and is inert until window_init.
heap.rs 6/6: crate-internal facade window_init/window_bytes; window_bytes doc now states data-page-only.
lib.rs: safe facade heap_window_init + heap_window_mapped_bytes (documented for #![deny(missing_docs)]); mapped_bytes doc clarified to DATA pages only (heap-grow-soundness minor).
arch/mod.rs: re-export heap_window + map_heap_frames from the x86_64 arm.
arch/mod.rs: re-export heap_window + map_heap_frames from the aarch64 arm (uniform per-arch contract).

edits.old_string: pub use mmu::{mmu_init, mmu_selftest};
edits.new_string: pub use mmu::{heap_window, map_heap_frames, mmu_init, mmu_selftest};
edits.note: arch/x86_64/mod.rs: surface the new mmu primitives.

edits.old_string: pub use mmu::{mmu_init, mmu_selftest};
edits.new_string: pub use mmu::{heap_window, map_heap_frames, mmu_init, mmu_selftest};
edits.note: arch/aarch64/mod.rs: surface the new mmu primitives.
x86_64/mmu.rs 1/3: import the M3 typed-layer helpers used by the M7 walk (SHIFT_512G is used at the PML4 level).
x86_64/mmu.rs 2/3: PTE_NX constant for the RW+NX heap leaves.

x86_64/mmu.rs 3/3: heap_window + map_heap_frames (+ zero_table/ensure_table/map_one_heap_page). Splice PML4[2] kernel-only RW+NX, intermediate tables from M6, no invlpg (0->1). FINALIZED: CR3 read hoisted out of map_one_heap_page into map_heap_frames (one read per grow); doc states no-half-mapped-page + interior-table retention.
aarch64/mmu.rs 1/3: import the M3 typed-layer helpers for the 3-level M7 walk (no SHIFT_512G — aarch64 is 39-bit/3-level).

edits.old_string:
/// 4 KiB Normal leaf at L3: page | AF | SH | attr1 (= 0x707 over the PA).
const PAGE_NORMAL: u64 = DESC_PAGE | DESC_AF | DESC_SH_INNER | DESC_ATTRIDX_NORMAL;
edits.note: aarch64/mmu.rs 2/3: PXN/UXN, TTBR_BADDR_MASK and the kernel-only RW+NX leaf template.

aarch64/mmu.rs 3/3: heap_window + read_ttbr0_el1 + map_heap_frames (+ helpers). Splice L1[4] kernel-only RW+NX via TTBR0_EL1, intermediate L2/L3 from M6, batch dsb ishst;isb, no TLBI (first-map). FINALIZED: TTBR0_EL1 read hoisted out of map_one_heap_page (one read per grow); doc states no-half-mapped-page + interior-table retention.

kernel/src/main.rs: M6 step-5 now RECLAIMS the drained pool (comment documents the arena-bound assumption) + the M7 self-test (NO unsafe): contiguous 4 MiB alloc proves scale-past-arena + contiguous-VA + frames consumed, a second 4 MiB alloc proves reuse without fresh frames (the strict no-frame-leak evidence), a realloc-path Vec exercises the unchanged M5 algebra, no heap leak after every drop, retained-frame note cites the sanctioned clause, then 'M7: heap OK'.

edits.old_string: MARKER='M6: frame alloc OK'
edits.new_string: MARKER='M7: heap OK'
edits.note: run-x86_64.sh: advance the DoD MARKER to M7 (the kernel still prints M0..M6 ahead of it in the same boot).

edits.old_string:
# scripts/run-x86_64.sh - QEMU launcher + Definition-of-Done check for M4 (x86_64).
#
# Boots the PVH ELF kernel under QEMU 'microvm' (the machine type Firecracker is
# modelled on), wires legacy 16550 COM1 to stdio, and asserts the EXACT marker
# "M5: alloc OK" appears on serial (the newest cumulative-boot milestone; the
# kernel drops to ring 3, the user stub issues `int 0x80`, the kernel handles it
# and returns). M0's hello, M1's trap round-trip, M2's ping-pong and M3's
# "M3: mmu OK" all print earlier in the same boot, so one run proves every
# milestone. Fail-closed: a wall-clock timeout bounds the run (the kernel halts
# rather than exiting), and a missing marker is a non-zero exit.
edits.new_string:
# scripts/run-x86_64.sh - QEMU launcher + Definition-of-Done check for M7 (x86_64).
#
# Boots the PVH ELF kernel under QEMU 'microvm' (the machine type Firecracker is
# modelled on), wires legacy 16550 COM1 to stdio, and asserts the EXACT marker
# "M7: heap OK" appears on serial (the newest cumulative-boot milestone; the
# kernel grows its frame-backed heap past the fixed 2 MiB arena and proves it).
# M0's hello, M1's trap round-trip, M2's ping-pong, M3's "M3: mmu OK", M4's
# "M4: user/ring OK", M5's "M5: alloc OK" and M6's "M6: frame alloc OK" all
# print earlier in the same boot, so one run proves every milestone. Fail-closed:
# a wall-clock timeout bounds the run (the kernel halts rather than exiting), and
# a missing marker is a non-zero exit.
edits.note: run-x86_64.sh: refresh the stale header comment (named M4/"M5: alloc OK") to the M7 DoD; matches the live MARKER. Cosmetic minor flagged by the framekernel review.

edits.old_string: MARKER="M6: frame alloc OK"
edits.new_string: MARKER="M7: heap OK"
edits.note: run-aarch64.sh: advance the DoD MARKER to M7.

edits.old_string:
# scripts/run-aarch64.sh -- QEMU `virt` runner (milestone M4: user/ring boundary).
#
# Boots the aarch64 tb-os image under QEMU, captures the PL011 serial stream,
# and asserts the executable Definition-of-Done marker "M5: alloc OK" (the
# EL0 round-trip proof: the kernel `eret`s to EL0, the user stub issues
# `svc #0`, the kernel handles it and returns). M0's hello, M1's trap
# round-trip, M2's ping-pong, "mmu-test: enabled, serial alive" and "M3: mmu OK"
# all print earlier in the same boot.
edits.new_string:
# scripts/run-aarch64.sh -- QEMU `virt` runner (milestone M7: frame-backed heap).
#
# Boots the aarch64 tb-os image under QEMU, captures the PL011 serial stream,
# and asserts the executable Definition-of-Done marker "M7: heap OK" (the
# kernel grows its frame-backed heap past the fixed 2 MiB arena and proves it).
# M0's hello, M1's trap round-trip, M2's ping-pong, "M3: mmu OK",
# "M4: user/ring OK", "M5: alloc OK" and "M6: frame alloc OK" all print earlier
# in the same boot.
edits.note: run-aarch64.sh: refresh the stale header comment to the M7 DoD; matches the live MARKER.

edits.old_string: MARKER='M6: frame alloc OK'
edits.new_string: MARKER='M7: heap OK'
edits.note: run-vmm-x86_64.sh: advance the tb-vmm/tb-boot DoD MARKER to M7.

edits.old_string:
# scripts/run-vmm-x86_64.sh — tb-vmm Definition-of-Done check (L1, x86_64).
#
# Boots the SAME kernel ELF the QEMU/PVH path boots, but through tb-vmm + the
# tb-boot v0 contract (direct 64-bit long mode, NO PVH note, NO A0 trampoline),
# and asserts the exact marker "M5: alloc OK" on the guest serial console.
# Requires a usable /dev/kvm; if absent it SKIPS (exit 0) with a clear message.
edits.new_string:
# scripts/run-vmm-x86_64.sh — tb-vmm Definition-of-Done check (L1, x86_64).
#
# Boots the SAME kernel ELF the QEMU/PVH path boots, but through tb-vmm + the
# tb-boot v0 contract (direct 64-bit long mode, NO PVH note, NO A0 trampoline),
# and asserts the exact marker "M7: heap OK" on the guest serial console (M0-M6,
# including "M6: frame alloc OK", all print earlier in the same boot).
# Requires a usable /dev/kvm; if absent it SKIPS (exit 0) with a clear message.
edits.note: run-vmm-x86_64.sh: refresh the stale header comment to the M7 DoD; matches the live MARKER.

edits.old_string: FINAL_MARKER="M6: frame alloc OK"
edits.new_string: FINAL_MARKER="M7: heap OK"
edits.note: bench-boot.sh: THE MAJOR — advance the live FINAL_MARKER M6->M7 so the vmm-boot.yml KVM boot-time read-loop times the WHOLE M7 self-test (it previously broke at M6, excluding the very milestone it should measure). Non-gating but a stale CI artifact the draft missed.

edits.old_string: #   * t_full   = spawn -> the final cumulative milestone marker ("M5: alloc OK").
edits.new_string: #   * t_full   = spawn -> the final cumulative milestone marker ("M7: heap OK").
edits.note: bench-boot.sh: refresh the t_full header comment (named the even-staler "M5: alloc OK") to match the corrected FINAL_MARKER.
